critic.verdict: An exceptionally well-crafted Easy chart with superb vocal-following rhythms and natural flow, though a couple of patterns might stretch the physical reading limits of a beginner.

issues.problem: The transition from JUMP LR to HOLD U is an 8th-note (0.273s) gap, requiring the player to immediately shift a foot from the outer panels to the top panel right after landing a jump, which can be challenging for Easy players.
Staggered overlapping holds (HOLD U starting at 73.909, HOLD R starting at 74.182) introduce multi-tasking coordination that is slightly advanced for an Easy rating.

critic.verdict: The chart captures the song's structure and energy well, but violates standard single-play design conventions with an illegal 3-panel hold and excessive difficulty spikes for a Medium rating.

issues.problem: Illegal 3-panel hold (D, U, and L held simultaneously) which requires hands or complex bracket-pressing, violating single-play pad guidelines, especially on Medium.
A continuous 10-second eighth-note stream featuring frequent jumps is too physically demanding and complex for a 'Medium' difficulty, causing a severe pacing and difficulty spike.
An awkward sixteenth-note tap leading directly into an eighth-note jump (JUMP LR) which is mechanically jarring and too advanced for this difficulty tier.
Rhythmically complex sixteenth-note tap occurring during an active hold, ending with a simultaneous release/tap, which is highly unintuitive for Medium-level players.

critic.verdict: A rhythmic and well-aligned chart that is highly playable but suffers from severe pattern repetition in the climax and a couple of awkward transitions.

issues.problem: The 4-note pattern 'JUMP LR -> U -> D -> L' is repeated 8 times consecutively. While it matches the synth loop, repeating the exact same physical movement so many times feels lazy, monotonous, and causes undue fatigue on the right foot.

Tapping U -> D -> L at 16th-note speed (0.136s intervals) right after a jump creates an uncomfortable transition, forcing either an awkward crossover or a very rapid, double-step on the left foot from D to L.

Tapping U at 68.725 while holding D until 68.862 is an awkward timing window. Releasing the hold a mere 16th-note after tapping with the other foot feels clunky and often leads to premature release errors.
